Title: γ-Aminobutyric acid plays a key role in alleviating Glomerella leaf spot in apples

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3 (a variant of cv. Royal Gala); Fuji; Orin calli
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher expression than MdGAD2 and MdGAD3 in roots, stems, leaves, and fruits; strongly induced after Colletotrichum fructicola inoculation in resistant cultivar Fuji
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots, stems, leaves, and fruits; up-regulated ~25-fold at 24 h after C. fructicola inoculation in Fuji leaves
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Glutamate-to-GABA entry step of the GABA shunt
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Amino acid derivative / non-protein amino acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances resistance to Glomerella leaf spot by improving antioxidant capacity and reducing ROS accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control gene for GABA synthesis in apple; predominant GAD identified in this study
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Colletotrichum fructicola inoculation causing Glomerella leaf spot; exogenous GABA treatment; transient transformation; callus infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; tissue expression profiling; correlation across 28 germplasms; in vitro enzyme assay by LC-MS; overexpression in apple calli; transient overexpression and RNAi in apple leaves; metabolite profiling; pathogen inoculation; ROS and antioxidant enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GABA accumulation and enhances resistance to Glomerella leaf spot, reducing lesion length and ROS while increasing SOD, CAT, and POD activities
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdGAD1 was identified as the major GABA synthetase in apple based on tissue expression, germplasm correlation, and in vitro enzymatic activity. 2. MdGAD1 overexpression increased GABA content in apple calli and leaves, whereas RNAi reduced GABA accumulation. 3. MdGAD1 positively improved resistance to Colletotrichum fructicola/GLS by enhancing antioxidant capacity and lowering ROS. 4. MdGAD1 acts downstream of MdWRKY33 in the GLS-resistance regulatory module.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering higher endogenous GABA and improved fungal disease resistance in apple; could be stacked with upstream activator MdWRKY33 for stronger GLS resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among MdGAD1/2/3, MdGAD1 showed the strongest correlation with GABA content (Pearson 0.808) and strongest induction relevant to GLS resistance; MdGAD1-GFP cytoplasmic signal was observed after 1 M sorbitol-induced plasmolysis

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3 (a variant of cv. Royal Gala); Fuji; Orin calli
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Colletotrichum fructicola; positively correlated with GABA content across 28 apple germplasms
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated ~20-fold 4 days after C. fructicola inoculation in Fuji leaves; transgene expression in apple calli and transiently transformed leaves
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor family; WRKY DNA-binding domain recognizing W-box elements
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Colletotrichum fructicola infection / Glomerella leaf spot
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MdGAD1 transcription by directly binding W-box motifs in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GABA biosynthesis via transcriptional activation of the GABA shunt entry enzyme
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Amino acid derivative / non-protein amino acid
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances resistance to Glomerella leaf spot by improving antioxidant capacity and reducing ROS accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive upstream regulator of GABA accumulation under GLS conditions through direct activation of MdGAD1
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Colletotrichum fructicola inoculation causing Glomerella leaf spot; transient transformation; callus infection assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid screening; EMSA; GUS reporter assay; dual-luciferase assay; subcellular localization; overexpression in apple calli; transient overexpression and RNAi in apple leaves; metabolite profiling; pathogen inoculation; genetic interaction/co-expression with MdGAD1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GABA accumulation and increases resistance to Glomerella leaf spot, reducing lesion length and ROS while increasing antioxidant enzyme activities
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdWRKY33 was identified by yeast one-hybrid as an upstream factor binding the MdGAD1 promoter. 2. EMSA, GUS, and LUC assays demonstrated that MdWRKY33 directly binds the MdGAD1 promoter W-box and activates transcription. 3. MdWRKY33 overexpression increased GABA accumulation in apple calli and leaves, while RNAi had the opposite effect. 4. MdWRKY33 positively regulates GLS resistance largely through the MdGAD1-mediated GABA biosynthetic pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulatory target for breeding or engineering disease-resistant, high-GABA apple germplasm; may be combined with MdGAD1 overexpression/editing for stronger effect
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdGAD2 and MdGAD3 were also up-regulated in MdWRKY33 overexpression lines and their promoters contain W-box elements, but MdGAD1 showed the strongest induction and clearest functional support in GLS resistance
